title: TodayView brief reader
description: Replace the stub in AggregatorApp/Today/TodayView.swift with the full brief reader. Wrap content in a NavigationStack with .navigationTitle("Today"). State: brief: Brief?, a phase enum (loading/loaded/error/empty), safariURL: URL? for external ref sheets. Guards: when !credentialsStore.isConfigured show the ContentUnavailableView pointing to Settings (matching SourcesView pattern). On .task: call getTodayBrief(); success → .loaded; APIError.http(404) → .empty; cancellation (isCancellation helper) → ignore; other errors → .error. Empty state: ContentUnavailableView with sparkles image. Loading: centered ProgressView. Error: message + Retry button. Loaded: ScrollView with ReaderLayout.hPadding, headline (.title2/.bold, fallback "Daily Brief"), caption with periodStart formatted via DateDisplay.mediumDate + model when present, intro via ParagraphText when present, topics in position order via BriefTopicView. Pull-to-refresh (.refreshable) re-fetches. Sheet presenting SafariView for safariURL.
acceptance_criteria: TodayView wraps content in NavigationStack with .navigationTitle("Today")
Not-configured state shows ContentUnavailableView directing to Settings
Loading shows centered ProgressView; error shows message + Retry; empty shows sparkles ContentUnavailableView
Loaded state renders headline (fallback to "Daily Brief"), date caption (periodStart via mediumDate + model), intro via ParagraphText, and topics in position order
External ref URL sheet presents SafariView; internal refs navigate via BriefTopicView NavigationLink
Cancelled fetch does not set error state
No hardcoded hex colors; Liquid Glass conventions preserved

handoff_summary.completed: Replaced TodayView stub with full implementation: NavigationStack with .navigationTitle("Today"); credentials guard (ContentUnavailableView → Settings when !isConfigured); Phase enum (loading/loaded/error/empty); fetchBrief() with 404→empty, cancellation→ignore, other errors→.error; ScrollView loaded state with headline (.title2/.bold, fallback "Daily Brief"), date+model caption via DateDisplay.mediumDate, intro via ParagraphText, topics sorted by position via BriefTopicView; .refreshable re-fetches; .sheet(isPresented:) presenting SafariView for safariURL; onExternalRef callback wires BriefTopicView external links to sheet.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. Uses only existing APIs (APIClient.getTodayBrief, DateDisplay.mediumDate, ParagraphText, BriefTopicView, ReaderLayout.hPadding, SafariView) all introduced by dependency beads.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify all acceptance criteria: credentials guard renders correctly, all four phase states render as expected, pull-to-refresh triggers a re-fetch, external ref sheet opens SafariView, internal refs navigate via ArticleDetailView, and 404 produces empty state not error state.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts — TodayView.swift was the only file modified and had no concurrent bead activity.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Phase enum with associated Error value (loading/loaded/error/empty) — chosen over a tri-state Optional approach (sources pattern) because the brief has four semantically distinct states: the 404 empty state is not an error and must be differentiated from a true error; an explicit enum makes that separation clear and exhaustive at the call site. 2. Sheet presentation via Binding(get:set:) on URL? rather than an IdentifiableURL wrapper — avoids a retroactive conformance or an extra struct; the sheet content guard (`if let url = safariURL`) handles the optional safely. 3. `captionString(_:)` returns a plain String rather than a SwiftUI Text concat — keeps the helper pure and testable without SwiftUI. 4. Topics sorted inline with `.sorted { $0.position < $1.position }` rather than a computed property — the sort is local to this one call site and does not warrant a helper.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) when credentialsStore.isConfigured == false → ContentUnavailableView with gearshape is shown; (2) during fetch → ProgressView shown; (3) 404 response → sparkles ContentUnavailableView shown (not error state); (4) other errors → error message + Retry button shown; (5) successful fetch → headline (or "Daily Brief" fallback), date caption with mediumDate, optional model appended with " · ", optional intro rendered via ParagraphText, topics rendered in ascending position order; (6) pull-to-refresh triggers a second fetch; (7) tapping an external ref in BriefTopicView opens the SafariView sheet; (8) tapping an internal ref navigates via NavigationLink to ArticleDetailView; (9) cancelled task does not flip phase to .error.

conflict_risks: No conflicts — TodayView.swift was the only file modified and had no concurrent bead activity.

execution_history.summary: Implemented the full TodayView brief reader, replacing the stub with all required states (loading, loaded, error, empty), pull-to-refresh, external ref sheet presentation via SafariView, and credentials guard matching the SourcesView pattern.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
